report_name: UK Cyber Survey: 43% of Businesses Breached, But Only 25% Have an IR Plan

identities.name: UK government
identities.description: The entity responsible for the annual Cyber Security Breaches Survey, providing an evidence base for future cybersecurity policy.

identities.name: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
identities.description: The UK government department that published the 2025/2026 Cyber Security Breaches Survey.

identities.name: National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC)
identities.description: The UK's national technical authority for cyber security, whose work and guidance are informed by the survey's findings.

identities.name: Information Commissioner's Office (ICO)
identities.description: The UK regulator responsible for enforcing GDPR, which may issue fines for data breaches such as those documented in the survey.

identities.name: UK businesses
identities.description: A class of UK organizations surveyed, 43% of which experienced a breach in the past year, with phishing being the most common attack vector (implicated in 85% of incidents). The survey found critical gaps in preparedness, with only 25% having a formal incident response plan, 15% managing supply chain risk, and low adoption of security practices for AI.

identities.name: UK charities
identities.description: A class of UK organizations surveyed, 28% of which suffered a cyber breach in the last year.

identities.name: Transport and storage sector
identities.description: A specific UK business sector that reported a 45% breach rate, slightly higher than the average for all businesses.

relationships.source_name: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT)
